title: Several advertised CLI flags are silently ignored

evidence.quote: `prompt`, `set-html`, and `navigate` all inherit policy flags, but only some handlers read them

reasoning: The CLI surface promises behaviors that the handlers do not implement. `open` accepts `--watch` but never reads it. `navigate` inherits `--allow-bridge`, `--allow-remote-resources`, and `--csp`, but its action only uses `o.allowRemote`. `prompt` and `set-html` also accept `--allow-bridge`, but their handlers never branch on that option. Because Commander accepts these flags and the command still returns success, callers can believe a policy or watch mode was applied when it was actually ignored.
recommendation: Remove unsupported flags so the CLI rejects them, or implement the missing behavior and assert that each parsed option changes the outgoing request/prompt payload.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked command-level tests assert option propagation or rejection, so no-op flags can ship unnoticed.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add CLI tests for each command that pass these flags and verify either a changed payload (`request`/`promptWindow` arguments) or an explicit usage error for unsupported options.

history.reasoning: The original evidence still exists in current src/cli.ts at lines 19 and 24-27. addPolicy still advertises --allow-remote, --allow-bridge, --allow-remote-resources, and --csp; open still advertises --watch on line 25 but no current source/test reference reads o.watch. navigate still inherits all policy flags on line 27 but its action only uses o.allowRemote and sends {window,url}, so --allow-bridge, --allow-remote-resources, and --csp remain accepted no-ops. prompt and set-html still accept --allow-bridge via addHtml/addPolicy without reading o.allowBridge. Help output confirms these flags are still advertised. The repository tests and typecheck pass, but they do not cover option propagation or rejection for these flags, so passing tests do not fix the contract mismatch.
commands: pwd && rg -n "addPolicy|allow-remote|allow-bridge|allow-remote-resources|csp|watch|prompt|set-html|navigate" src test tests spec 2>/dev/null
bun src/cli.ts prompt --help && bun src/cli.ts set-html --help

history.reasoning: Current src/cli.ts still exists, but the original addPolicy evidence moved/changed. The old single addPolicy helper has been split into addUrlPolicy/addHtmlPolicy/addOpenPolicy/addPromptPolicy at src/cli.ts:19-22. The navigate and set-html policy-surface parts appear fixed: navigate now only advertises --allow-remote, set-html no longer advertises --allow-bridge, and test/cli-options.test.ts asserts unsupported policy flags are rejected. prompt now reads --allow-bridge for remote URL prompts and applies csp/allowRemoteResources. However, open still advertises --watch at src/cli.ts:28 and in help output, and rg finds no o.watch handling or watch implementation in src/test beyond that option declaration. bun test and bun run typecheck pass, but they do not cover --watch propagation/rejection. Because one advertised CLI flag from the original finding remains accepted and ignored, the issue is still open, narrowed to open --watch.
commands: bun src/cli.ts open --help && bun src/cli.ts navigate --help && bun src/cli.ts prompt --help && bun src/cli.ts set-html --help

history.reasoning: The original src/cli.ts evidence no longer exists as described: the old addPolicy helper was split into addUrlPolicy/addHtmlPolicy/addOpenPolicy/addPromptPolicy at src/cli.ts:20-23. The current command surfaces now match current handling. navigate only advertises --allow-remote and rejects --csp as an unknown option; set-html advertises only HTML policy flags it uses; prompt reads allowBridge/csp/allowRemoteResources; open reads o.watch, rejects non-file sources, passes source.watch and watchPath in the open request, and the daemon consumes watchPath in WindowRegistry.open via watchHtmlFile. dist/cli.mjs and dist/daemon-main.mjs contain the same implementation. Tests pass, including cli-options tests for rejected unsupported flags and open --watch usage, plus watcher reload behavior. The prior accepted-but-ignored flag issue is fixed.
commands: bun src/cli.ts open --help && bun src/cli.ts navigate --help && bun src/cli.ts prompt --help && bun src/cli.ts set-html --help
